description: Refactor the I2C simulation stack to protocol-level primitives (start, stop, repeated_start, send_byte, recv_byte). Add a ProtocolInterpreter that groups protocol steps into master transactions, expose new ops through the backend API, and add 5 new low-level nodes to the React Flow frontend.

userStories.title: Protocol Step Interpreter
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a protocol step interpreter in the driver layer so that protocol-level step sequences can be translated into master transactions.
acceptanceCriteria: New class ProtocolInterpreter in backend/sim/protocol_interpreter.py
interpret(steps) method groups protocol steps into Transaction dataclass list

Grouping rules: start begins group, first send_byte is addr+RW byte (LSB=RW), subsequent send_bytes are data, recv_byte collects read bytes with ack param, repeated_start ends group with repeated_start=true, stop ends group with repeated_start=false
Transaction dataclass with fields: addr (int), rw (0=write, 1=read), data_bytes (list[int]), read_count (int), repeated_start (bool)
Raises ValueError for invalid sequences (send_byte without start, recv_byte in write mode, etc.)
Unit tests (pytest, no cocotb): write sequence, read sequence, write-then-read with repeated start, validation errors
Typecheck passes (mypy)
userStories.notes:
The RTL master is transaction-based: START + ADDR + N data bytes + STOP/REPEATED_START as one atomic op. The interpreter must group protocol-level steps into these transactions.

Address byte convention: first send_byte after start is addr+RW. Bits [7:1] = 7-bit slave addr, Bit [0] = RW (0=write, 1=read). Example: 0xA0 = addr 0x50 write, 0xA1 = addr 0x50 read.

Grouping example:
  start → send_byte(0xA0) → send_byte(0x10) → repeated_start → send_byte(0xA1) → recv_byte(ACK) → recv_byte(NACK) → stop
  → Transaction 1: addr=0x50, rw=WRITE, data=[0x10], repeated_start=true
  → Transaction 2: addr=0x50, rw=READ, read_count=2, repeated_start=false

Master hardware constraint: num_bytes is 4-bit (max 15). Write max = 14 data bytes (1 slot for reg pointer). Read max = 15 bytes. The interpreter should handle chunking for long sequences.

userStories.title: Driver Execute Transaction List
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the I2CDriver to execute a list of Transaction objects so that interpreted protocol steps can run on the hardware.
acceptanceCriteria: New method I2CDriver.execute_transactions(txns: list[Transaction]) -> list[TxnResult]
For write transactions: drives slave_addr_in, rw=0, num_bytes, data_in, repeated_start_in — reuses existing payload-feeding logic
For read transactions: drives slave_addr_in, rw=1, num_bytes, repeated_start_in — reuses existing data_valid capture logic
repeated_start_in set to 1 when txn.repeated_start is True
TxnResult dataclass: ack_ok (bool), data_read (list[int]), bytes_written (int)
Existing write_bytes/read_bytes/scan methods remain unchanged and functional
Typecheck passes (mypy)

sharedFiles.reason: Adding new execute_transactions method and importing Transaction/TxnResult

userStories.notes:
Key DUT signals: slave_addr_in, rw, num_bytes, data_in, repeated_start_in, start, busy, done, ack_error, data_out, data_valid, byte_count.

Extract the payload-feeding logic from write_bytes (lines 143-246) and the data_valid capture from read_bytes (lines 293-359) into internal helpers that execute_transactions can reuse. The repeated_start_in signal on line 90/158/301 is currently always set to 0 — this is the signal to drive.

Inter-transaction gap: use ClockCycles(dut.clk, 10) between transactions, UNLESS the previous transaction ended with repeated_start (in that case, no gap — the master transitions directly to the next START).

userStories.title: Cocotb Tests for Repeated Start
userStories.description: As a developer, I want cocotb tests that exercise repeated start sequences so that I can verify the RTL + driver work correctly end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: New test file backend/sim/tests/test_protocol.py
Test: write register pointer (1 byte) → repeated start → read N bytes (classic EEPROM pattern)
Test: write data → repeated start → read back same data (verify register contents match)
Test: scan via write with repeated start (addr probe without STOP)
Test: multi-segment repeated start chain (write → RS → read → RS → write → STOP)
All tests use ProtocolInterpreter + I2CDriver.execute_transactions()
All tests pass with cocotb + Icarus Verilog
Verify via register dump that data integrity is maintained across repeated start sequences
userStories.notes:
Run tests with: cd backend/sim && python -m pytest tests/test_protocol.py (cocotb runner pattern, same as test_i2c_cocotb.py).

The verilog_practice/I2C/i2c_master_tb.v has a repeated_start_test task (lines 332-374) as reference for expected behavior.

Key test pattern — classic EEPROM read:
  1. Start → send_byte(0xA0) → send_byte(reg_addr) → repeated_start
  2. send_byte(0xA1) → recv_byte(ACK) → recv_byte(NACK) → stop
  3. Verify read data matches what was written to reg_addr

The slave's in_read_ack_window fix (already in i2c_demo RTL) prevents false STOP detection during read ACK phase, which is critical for repeated start reliability.

userStories.title: Protocol-Level Step Ops in Test Runner
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the test_runner to support protocol-level ops so that JSON test sequences can use fine-grained I2C steps.
acceptanceCriteria: New ops in test_runner.py parse_step()/execute_step(): start (no params), stop (no params), repeated_start (no params), send_byte (data: int|hex-string), recv_byte (ack: bool)
Protocol steps are buffered until stop is encountered, then passed to ProtocolInterpreter.interpret() and executed via execute_transactions()
Each send_byte/recv_byte within a start..stop block gets its own result entry with status ok/fail and relevant data
Existing ops (reset, write_bytes, read_bytes, scan, delay) continue to work unchanged
Typecheck passes (mypy)

sharedFiles.reason: Adding new op parsing and protocol buffering logic to parse_step/execute_step

userStories.notes:
Buffering strategy: when execute_step encounters op='start', it enters protocol-buffering mode. Subsequent steps (send_byte, recv_byte, repeated_start) are collected into a buffer. When op='stop' is encountered, the full buffer is passed to ProtocolInterpreter.interpret(), then I2CDriver.execute_transactions(). Results are mapped back: each send_byte/recv_byte gets its own step result.

JSON example:
  {"steps": [
    {"op": "reset"},
    {"op": "start"},
    {"op": "send_byte", "data": "0xA0"},
    {"op": "send_byte", "data": "0x10"},
    {"op": "repeated_start"},
    {"op": "send_byte", "data": "0xA1"},
    {"op": "recv_byte", "ack": true},
    {"op": "recv_byte", "ack": false},
    {"op": "stop"}
  ]}

The execute_step function will need a stateful context (or modify execute_sequence) to handle buffering across multiple step calls.

userStories.title: Protocol Sequence Validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want protocol step sequences to be validated before execution so that malformed sequences produce clear error messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Validation function validate_protocol_sequence(steps) -> list[str] in protocol_interpreter.py
Validates: every start has a matching stop or repeated_start before next start
Validates: send_byte/recv_byte only appear between start and stop/repeated_start
Validates: first send_byte after start is a valid address byte (0x00-0xFF)
Validates: recv_byte only in read mode (address byte LSB=1)
Validates: send_byte (after address byte) only in write mode (address byte LSB=0)
Returns list of human-readable error strings (empty = valid)
Backend returns 422 with validation errors if protocol sequence is invalid
Unit tests for each validation rule
Typecheck passes (mypy)

sharedFiles.reason: Adding validate_protocol_sequence function to existing module

userStories.notes:
This can run in parallel with US-004 since US-005 only adds a validation function to protocol_interpreter.py (append-only) while US-004 modifies test_runner.py.

Validation should be called in the backend route handler (backend/app/routes/simulation.py) before passing steps to the test runner. If validation fails, return HTTP 422 with the error list.

Edge cases to validate:
- Empty sequence (no start/stop) → valid (just means no protocol steps)
- start immediately followed by stop → invalid (no address byte)
- Multiple starts without stop → invalid
- recv_byte after send_byte(addr+W) → invalid (wrong mode)
- send_byte after send_byte(addr+R) → invalid (should be recv_byte in read mode)

userStories.title: Integration Test — Protocol Sequences via API
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify protocol-level sequences work end-to-end through the FastAPI backend.
acceptanceCriteria: New template JSON: backend/sim/templates/repeated_start_read.json — write register pointer → repeated start → read pattern
Test: POST /api/run with protocol-level steps returns correct results
Test: POST /api/run with mixed protocol + legacy ops in same sequence works
Test: POST /api/run with invalid protocol sequence returns 422 with error details
All tests pass
userStories.notes:
Template JSON for repeated_start_read.json:
  {"name": "Repeated Start Read", "description": "Write register pointer then read via repeated start", "steps": [
    {"op": "reset"},
    {"op": "write_bytes", "addr": "0x50", "reg": "0x00", "data": ["0xDE", "0xAD"]},
    {"op": "start"},
    {"op": "send_byte", "data": "0xA0"},
    {"op": "send_byte", "data": "0x00"},
    {"op": "repeated_start"},
    {"op": "send_byte", "data": "0xA1"},
    {"op": "recv_byte", "ack": true},
    {"op": "recv_byte", "ack": false},
    {"op": "stop"}
  ]}

Mixed test: combine legacy write_bytes with protocol-level read via repeated start in one sequence.

userStories.title: Protocol Control Nodes (Start, Stop, RepeatedStart)
userStories.description: As a user, I want Start, Stop, and RepeatedStart nodes on the canvas so that I can control I2C bus protocol flow.
acceptanceCriteria: StartNode — displays 'START' label, green accent, one output handle (bottom), no input handle
StopNode — displays 'STOP' label, red accent, one input handle (top), no output handle
RepeatedStartNode — displays 'Sr' label, orange accent, one input handle (top), one output handle (bottom)
All three registered in nodeTypes map in frontend/src/components/nodes/index.ts
Sidebar updated with new 'Protocol' section showing these 3 node types as draggable items
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: all 3 nodes render correctly with handles

sharedFiles.reason: Registering new node types in nodeTypes map
Adding Protocol section to sidebar layout

userStories.notes:
Create StartNode.tsx, StopNode.tsx, RepeatedStartNode.tsx in frontend/src/components/nodes/.

Sidebar layout update: add a divider and 'Protocol' section header below the existing 5 Basic node types. Sidebar items use HTML5 dataTransfer with node type string.

Node type strings: 'i2c_start', 'i2c_stop', 'repeated_start'. Use 'i2c_start'/'i2c_stop' to avoid conflict with any React Flow internal 'start'/'stop' strings.

StartNode is similar to ResetNode (no params, just label + output handle). StopNode is similar but input handle only. RepeatedStartNode has both handles.

Colors: Start=emerald/green, Stop=rose/red, RepeatedStart=orange/amber.

userStories.title: Data Transfer Nodes (SendByte, RecvByte)
userStories.description: As a user, I want SendByte and RecvByte nodes so that I can specify individual bytes in an I2C transaction.
acceptanceCriteria: SendByteNode — inline hex input for byte value (0x00-0xFF), purple accent, input handle (top), output handle (bottom)
SendByteNode displays auto-computed helper text when value looks like address byte: 'Addr: 0x{byte>>1} {R/W}'
RecvByteNode — ACK/NACK toggle (select), teal accent, input handle (top), output handle (bottom), read-only data field shown after simulation
Both registered in nodeTypes map
Sidebar Protocol section updated with these 2 node types
Default values: SendByte data=0xA0, RecvByte ack=true
Validation: SendByte data must be valid hex 0x00-0xFF
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: both nodes render with correct fields

sharedFiles.reason: Registering SendByte and RecvByte node types
Adding 2 more items to Protocol section

userStories.notes:
Create SendByteNode.tsx and RecvByteNode.tsx in frontend/src/components/nodes/.

Node type strings: 'send_byte', 'recv_byte'.

SendByteNode helper text logic: parse the hex value, if it's a plausible address byte (any valid hex), show decoded addr and R/W. Example: input '0xA0' → 'Addr: 0x50 W', input '0xA1' → 'Addr: 0x50 R'. This is always shown as helper text (the user decides if it's actually an address byte based on position in chain).

RecvByteNode: the 'data' field is read-only and populated after simulation via node.data.receivedData (set by the status overlay logic in US-009's pattern). For now, just show an empty read-only field with placeholder 'After sim'.

Use existing validate.ts pattern: validateHexByte(v) for SendByte validation. Add 'nodrag' class on inputs.

userStories.title: Serialization for Protocol-Level Nodes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the flow serializer to handle protocol-level nodes so that protocol sequences can be sent to the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: serializeFlow updated to map: i2c_start→{op:'start'}, i2c_stop→{op:'stop'}, repeated_start→{op:'repeated_start'}, send_byte→{op:'send_byte',data:'0xNN'}, recv_byte→{op:'recv_byte',ack:bool}
Mixed flows with legacy + protocol nodes serialize correctly
Protocol nodes can coexist with legacy nodes in same flow (e.g., Reset → Start → SendByte → ... → Stop → Delay)
Unit tests: protocol-only chain, mixed chain, empty protocol section
Typecheck passes

sharedFiles.reason: Adding new node type cases to mapNodeToStep switch

userStories.title: Protocol Sequence Visual Indicators
userStories.description: As a user, I want visual feedback about protocol sequence validity on the canvas so that I can see errors before running.
acceptanceCriteria: Start and Stop nodes show warning icon if unpaired (Start without Stop in chain, or vice versa)
SendByte/RecvByte nodes show warning if not between Start and Stop in the chain
First SendByte after Start shows decoded address + R/W direction as helper text
RecvByte shows warning if used in write mode (address byte LSB=0)
Validation runs on every flow change and results stored in node.data.warning
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: warnings appear for invalid protocol sequences

sharedFiles.reason: Adding protocol validation logic that runs on flow change and updates node.data.warning

userStories.notes:
Create a validateProtocolFlow(nodes, edges) function in frontend/src/lib/validate.ts (or a new protocol-validate.ts). This walks the serialized chain and checks:
1. Every i2c_start has a matching i2c_stop (or chain ends)
2. send_byte/recv_byte only between i2c_start and i2c_stop
3. After i2c_start, first send_byte is address byte — decode and show R/W
4. recv_byte only valid when address byte LSB=1 (read mode)
5. send_byte (after addr) only valid when address byte LSB=0 (write mode)

Store warnings in node.data.warning (string or null). Node components should render a yellow warning icon + tooltip when data.warning is truthy.

Call validateProtocolFlow in FlowCanvas whenever nodes/edges change (same pattern as existing validation). Clear warnings when flow changes.
